rag_rules: Place output range guarantees in component type scopes using visible feature names and concrete bounds.
Use AGREE logical operators and temporal recurrence for stateful equations and branching logic.
Write guarantees that relate visible outputs to visible inputs; avoid implementation-only subcomponent paths.
Generate syntactically valid AGREE annex blocks with proper assume/guarantee structure.
generic_rules: Component type contracts should use visible variables and feature names for assumptions and guarantees.
Constants and equations in AGREE should match the types of the referenced AADL features.
Assignments in AGREE are valid only in implementation scopes after subcomponents and connections.
AGREE contracts must be enclosed in annex agree {** ... **}; blocks with valid AADL scope placement.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: Use `assume "label": expression;` and `guarantee "label": expression;` for contract clauses.
Declare helper equations with `eq name : type = expression;` and constants with `const name : type = value;`.
Use `if ... then ... else` for branching logic and `and`, `or`, `not(...)`, `=>` for logical expressions.
Ensure all visible identifiers are declared or accessible in the same annex or component scope.
anti_patterns: Do not reference AADL property names (e.g., Period, Deadline) as AGREE variables unless explicitly typed.
Do not place implementation-level bindings in component type annexes.
Do not use subcomponent ports in component type contracts unless represented through valid variables.
Do not generate guarantees that depend on implementation-only paths or unresolvable identifiers.
irrelevant_topics: AADL property associations and timing attributes
Model fusion and insertion location decisions
